en.levelText0: The smallest component of an element, which is a basic category of matter, is an atom.
en.levelText1: The amount of protons an element has is equal to its atomic number.
en.levelText2: Protons, neutrons, and electrons are subatomic particles that make up atoms.
en.levelText3: Atomic mass is the measurement of the mass of an element's atoms.
en.levelText4: Protons and neutrons are located in the nucleus of an atom.
en.levelText5: Protons have a positive charge and electrons have a negative charge.
en.levelText6: Electrons orbit the nucleus of an atom in an electron cloud.
en.levelText7: Noble gases and native elements are made of a single type of atom.
en.levelText8: An atom can also have a charge. If an atom has a charge of 1+, it means it has 1 less electron
en.levelText9: If an atom has a charge of 1-, it means it has 1 extra electron

en.help: The atomic number is equal to protons, electrons are equal to protons, and neutrons are equal to atomic mass less protons.
en.help2: The periodic notation above has this amount of proton, neutron and electron.
en.game1: Collect All the diamonds. So we can create a proton orb.
en.game2: Collect All the diamonds. So we can create a neutron orb.
en.game3: Collect All the diamonds. So we can create a electron orb.
en.sample1: The atomic number is equal to protons, electrons are equal to protons, and neutrons are equal to atomic mass less protons.
en.sample2: Electrons will be equal to protons plus the charge if there is a negative charge, but protons minus the charge if there is a positive charge.

en.howToPlay: This game is very easy to play. You're first introduced to a drag and drop game, Then a runner game where you dodge obstacles and hit the right boxes.
en.meek: Choose elements that correspond to the periodic notation.
en.dop1: Proton, stable subatomic particle that has a positive charge equal in magnitude to a unit of electron charge.
en.dop2: Neutron, neutral subatomic particle that is a constituent of every atomic nucleus except ordinary hydrogen.
en.dop3: An electron is a negatively charged subatomic particle. It can be either free (not attached to any atom), or bound to the nucleus of an atom
en.story1: Our galaxy was peaceful, and our earth was habitable.
en.story3: You have been chosen to build a planet by creating an artificial atom. Good Luck!
en.story2: Asteroids from a distant galaxy slammed into our universe, unleashing destruction.
en.story4: Great Work, You and Atomic boy have successfully created a new earth from the atom. Thank you for Playing..
en.desc_1: Use the computer on your left to unlock the doors.
en.desc_2: You will travel to proton, neutron and electron planets using the portals. GoodLuck
en.desc_3: Unlock them using the computer device by your left. Goodluck! Explorer.
en.desc_4: You have a proton orb. Put it in the atom machine on the right.
en.desc_5: Well Done! You possess a neutron orb. You are quite skilled at this.
en.desc_6: You possess an electron orb. Superb!
